q.en: What does the narrator notice happening every Sunday night for three months?
q.zh: 叙述者注意到连续三个月每个周日晚上发生了什么？

options.en: Maria practices violin according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: Maria练习小提琴（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: A group of people in similar dark uniforms carrying cases enters the house across the street
options.zh: 一群穿着相似深色制服、拿着箱子的人走进街对面的房子

explain.en: Alan says, 'I have seen it at the same house, at the same time, every Sunday night for the last three months... a group of individuals... wears a similar looking uniform and carries some kind of case.'
explain.zh: Alan说：“过去三个月里，每个周日晚上同一时间，我都在同一栋房子看到……一群人……穿着相似的制服，拿着某种箱子。”

q.en: Who is the narrator of the story?

options.en: Alan, a boy who moved from Boston
options.zh: Alan，一个从波士顿搬来的男孩

options.en: Eduardo according to some versions of the story

explain.en: Maria calls him 'Alan? What are you doing here?' and Alan notes he 'was born in Boston,' making him the narrator.
explain.zh: Maria喊他“Alan？你在这里做什么？”，而Alan提到自己“出生在波士顿”，说明他就是叙述者。

q.en: The loud, deep laugh 'shook me like a leaf' and sounded like 'an eerie Santa Claus.' What does this show?
q.zh: 那阵“把我震得像片叶子”的低沉大笑，听起来像“诡异的圣诞老人”。这说明了什么？

options.en: The laugh was deep and frightening, like a big man's
options.zh: 那笑声低沉又吓人，像是个高大男人的

options.en: It was a real Santa Claus according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 那是真正的圣诞老人（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says, 'One loud, deep laugh shook me like a leaf. It sounded like some kind of eerie Santa Claus. It must have come from a very big man.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“一阵响亮低沉的笑声把我震得像片叶子。听起来像某种诡异的圣诞老人。那一定来自一个很高大的男人。”

q.en: Why does Alan first not realize the people are musicians?
q.zh: 为什么Alan起初没有意识到那些人是乐手？

options.en: He thought they were thieves according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 他以为他们是小偷（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

options.en: It was dark and he saw cases but did not connect them to instruments until Maria showed her violin case
options.zh: 天很黑，他看到了箱子，却没把它们和乐器联系起来，直到Maria拿出小提琴盒

explain.en: When Maria shows her violin case, Alan thinks, 'Maybe I was too busy thinking about the mystery to make the connection,' explaining why he didn't realize they were musicians.
explain.zh: 当Maria拿出小提琴盒时，Alan想：“也许我太专注于这个谜团，没能把它们联系起来”，这解释了他为何没意识到那些人是乐手。

options.en: A Spanish word for thank you
options.zh: 西班牙语里“谢谢”的意思

explain.en: The glossary defines mariachi as 'traditional Mexican folk music and one or more members of a band that performs it.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将mariachi定义为“传统的墨西哥民间音乐，以及演奏它的乐队成员”。

q.en: How many musicians and what instruments are in Eduardo's mariachi group?
q.zh: Eduardo的流浪乐队里有几名乐手，演奏哪些乐器？

options.en: Three violins, three guitars, and two trumpets
options.zh: 三把小提琴、三把吉他和两支小号

options.en: One violin and one guitar according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一把小提琴和一把吉他（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Inside the house, 'Three members of the group played violins, another three played guitars, and two others played trumpets.'
explain.zh: 在屋里，“乐队里三位成员拉小提琴，另外三位弹吉他，还有两位吹小号。”

q.en: Which Spanish instrument words does Alan find easy to learn, and why?
q.zh: Alan觉得哪些西班牙语乐器词容易记，为什么？

options.en: Violín, guitarra, trompeta—because they look and sound like the English violin, guitar, trumpet
options.zh: violín、guitarra、trompeta——因为它们看起来、听起来都像英文的violin、guitar、trumpet

options.en: Las Mañanitas, because it is a song according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: Las Mañanitas，因为它是一首歌（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

explain.en: Alan says 'violín, guitarra, and trompeta were very easy to memorize because they look and sound a lot like the English words violin, guitar, and trumpet.'
explain.zh: Alan说“violín、guitarra和trompeta很好记，因为它们看起来、听起来都和英文的violin、guitar、trumpet很像。”

options.en: Musical according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 有音乐性的（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines eerie as 'strange or frightening.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将eerie定义为“奇怪或令人害怕的”。

q.en: What does Alan decide he wants to do after solving the mystery?
q.zh: 解开谜团后，Alan决定想做什么？

options.en: Take guitar lessons at school and maybe serenade Maria like a mariachi
options.zh: 在学校学吉他课，也许像流浪乐队成员一样去给Maria唱小夜曲

options.en: Learn to play the trumpet in Boston according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 在波士顿学吹小号（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Alan hopes 'Dad will let me take guitar lessons at school next year... I may buy a sombrero and go serenade Maria—just like a real mariachi!'
explain.zh: Alan希望“爸爸明年让我在学校学吉他课……我也许会买顶宽边帽，像真正的流浪乐队成员一样去给Maria唱小夜曲！”

q.en: What is the main point the author wants readers to take from this story?
q.zh: 作者希望读者从这个故事中得到的主要启示是什么？

options.en: That violins are better than guitars according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 小提琴比吉他好（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A mystery can lead to discovering and appreciating a new culture
options.zh: 一个谜团可以引导人去发现并欣赏一种新的文化

explain.en: The story concludes, 'As it turned out the mystery led me to discover new things about another culture. I want to learn more.'
explain.zh: 故事结尾写道：“结果这个谜团让我发现了另一种文化的新事物。我想了解更多。”

prompt.en: Pretend you are Alan writing a thank-you note to Maria's uncle Eduardo after hearing the mariachi. Tell him what you learned and how you felt.
prompt.zh: 假设你是Alan，听完流浪乐队演奏后给Maria的叔叔Eduardo写一封感谢信。告诉他你学到了什么，以及你的感受。

en: Mention at least one Spanish instrument word you learned (violín, guitarra, or trompeta).
Say how solving the mystery changed your view of your neighbors.
zh: 至少提到一个你学到的西班牙语乐器词（violín、guitarra或trompeta）。
说说解开谜团如何改变了你对邻居的看法。
